title: Adds --policy-insecure-allow-anything flag
body:
This patch adds a new flag --policy-insecure-allow-anything
Closes #181, we can now directly use the tool with the
above mentioned flag wihout using a policy file.

Signed-off-by: Kushal Das mail@kushaldas.in

comments.body:
I'm +1 on this (I don't want to have this in containers/image also)

@mtrmac wdyt?
Lets just call this --insecure. You guys must be better typers then I am.

I am a little uneasy with `--insecure` because it is so general; wouldn’t users expect this to also (only?) disable TLS certificate verification? (And then, would they use `--insecure` to bypass one check without noticing that they have disabled others?)

I would be happier with any of / any permutation of `--insecure-policy`, `--insecure-trust`, etc. Y’all are right that `accept-anything` is unnecessarily verbose.

(I am not good at UI, and I am perfectly willing to concede this to the consensus opinion; I just wanted to make sure that this issue has been considered.)

> would be happier with any of / any permutation of --insecure-policy, --insecure-trust, etc. Y’all are right that accept-anything is unnecessarily verbose.

I'm happy as well with either of those - @rhatdan do you have any preference?
--trust-all  @aweiteka wants us to talk about trust.

@kushaldas Sorry, it seems nobody has noticed your update. The code LGTM, the only outstanding question is how much discussion needs to happen around naming (the code does `--insecure-policy`).

(`docs/skopeo.1.md` will also need updating—_after_ there is a consensus on the naming.)
I would call it insecure-policy to remark that it's still insecure to run this way, trust-all makes me think of a secure policy which happens to allow anything.
Ok lets go with --insecure-policy  and update the man page and the bash completions.
What all needs to be done for this PR to be merged?
This PR needs a rebase and fixes from @rhatdan comment above
@kushaldas Could you update your pull request?
